questions.q: In which field of science did Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar primarily work?
questions.explanation: Bhatnagar was a chemist, working notably in colloid chemistry, magnetochemistry and industrial chemistry.

questions.q: Bhatnagar is widely regarded as the architect of which Indian organisation?
options: CSIR (Council of Scientific and Industrial Research)
questions.explanation: He built and led the network of national laboratories under the Council of Scientific and Industrial Research (CSIR).

questions.q: India's top prize for science and technology research is named after Bhatnagar. What is it called?
questions.explanation: The Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Prize for Science and Technology is among India's most prestigious science awards, named in his honour.

questions.q: Bhatnagar served as the first Director-General of which body?
questions.explanation: He was the first Director-General of the CSIR, shaping India's post-independence scientific research infrastructure.

questions.q: In which country was Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar born?
questions.explanation: Bhatnagar was born in 1894 in Bhera, in the Shahpur district of pre-partition British India (now in Pakistan).

questions.q: The Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Prize is awarded to scientists who are generally below what age?
questions.explanation: The SSB Prize recognises outstanding research by scientists who are generally under the age of 45.

questions.q: Which civilian honour was conferred on Bhatnagar by the Government of India in 1954?
questions.explanation: Bhatnagar was awarded the Padma Bhushan, then India's third-highest civilian honour, in 1954 (the first year the Padma awards were given).

questions.q: Apart from being a scientist, Bhatnagar was also known for his talent in which creative pursuit?
questions.explanation: Bhatnagar was a noted Urdu poet and even wrote the CSIR's anthem.

questions.q: The honorific title 'Sir' was conferred on Bhatnagar by which authority?
options: The Government of India after independence
questions.explanation: He was knighted by the British Crown in 1941, becoming Sir Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar.

questions.q: The network of laboratories Bhatnagar established was meant to serve mainly what purpose?
questions.explanation: The CSIR laboratories were created to advance industrial and applied scientific research for national development.

questions.q: In which year was Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar born?
questions.explanation: Bhatnagar was born on 21 February 1894.

questions.q: Bhatnagar earned his doctoral degree (DSc) from which university?
questions.explanation: He completed his DSc at University College London in 1921, working under the chemist Frederick G. Donnan.

questions.q: An instrument co-developed by Bhatnagar to measure magnetic properties of materials is known as the:
questions.explanation: With K. N. Mathur he developed the Bhatnagar-Mathur magnetic interference balance for measuring magnetic susceptibility.

questions.q: Which branch of chemistry was a major focus of Bhatnagar's research?
questions.explanation: Bhatnagar made significant contributions to magnetochemistry, the study of the magnetic properties of chemical substances.

questions.q: Before joining national institutions, Bhatnagar was a professor of chemistry at which university?
questions.explanation: He served as Professor of Chemistry at the University of the Punjab in Lahore from 1924.

questions.q: In which year was Bhatnagar elected a Fellow of the Royal Society (FRS)?
questions.explanation: Bhatnagar was elected a Fellow of the Royal Society of London in 1943.

questions.q: Bhatnagar's early industrial chemistry work famously helped solve a problem for which industry through an emulsion/mud solution?
questions.explanation: He developed a solution to a drilling-mud problem for an oil company, an early example of applied chemistry solving an industrial challenge.

questions.q: In which year did Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar pass away?
questions.explanation: Bhatnagar died on 1 January 1955.

questions.q: The Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Prize is conferred by which organisation?
questions.explanation: The SSB Prize is awarded annually by the CSIR, the body Bhatnagar founded and first led.

questions.q: In which year did the CSIR institute the Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Prize?
questions.explanation: The Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar Prize was instituted in 1958, a few years after his death.

questions.q: Besides leading the CSIR, Bhatnagar held which influential government scientific advisory role?
options: First Secretary to the Ministry of Education for science
He was associated with planning and advising on national scientific policy
Head of the Indian Army's research wing
Director of the Geological Survey of India
questions.explanation: Bhatnagar was deeply involved in shaping national science policy and the institutional framework for research in newly independent India.

questions.q: Which subtitle best captures Bhatnagar's legacy in India?
options: Father of the Indian Space Programme
Father of the Indian Atomic Bomb
questions.explanation: He is celebrated as the architect of India's research and development infrastructure through the CSIR network.

questions.q: Under whom did Bhatnagar carry out his doctoral research in London?
questions.explanation: Bhatnagar worked under Professor Frederick G. Donnan at University College London for his DSc.

questions.q: The Bhatnagar-Mathur magnetic interference balance was later manufactured and marketed in collaboration with which British firm?
questions.explanation: The instrument was put into commercial production with the British scientific-instrument maker Messrs. Adam Hilger Ltd of London.

questions.q: Bhatnagar completed his early schooling and intermediate education before traveling to England on which type of funding?
options: A scholarship (the Dyal Singh Trust / overseas scholarship)
questions.explanation: He went to England for higher studies on a scholarship, which enabled his doctoral work at UCL.

questions.q: Which premier CSIR laboratory, established during Bhatnagar's tenure, focuses on physical and chemical metrology and standards?
questions.explanation: The National Physical Laboratory in New Delhi was among the chain of national laboratories set up under Bhatnagar's leadership of CSIR.

questions.q: Bhatnagar's research on the influence of magnetic fields and related effects contributed to a phenomenon sometimes associated with his name in colloid and magnetochemistry. Which area best describes this?
options: Magneto-chemical studies of solutions and emulsions
questions.explanation: His work centred on magneto-chemical investigations of solutions, colloids and emulsions, a hallmark of his scientific output.

questions.q: During the Second World War period, Bhatnagar's applied research efforts were channeled to support which national need?
options: Wartime industrial and defence-related research for India
questions.explanation: His applied research and the Board of Scientific and Industrial Research he helped guide supported wartime industrial and defence-related needs, paving the way for CSIR.

questions.q: Which Indian Prime Minister was a strong supporter of Bhatnagar's vision for building scientific institutions after independence?
questions.explanation: Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru championed scientific institution-building and supported Bhatnagar's CSIR expansion.

questions.q: Roughly how many national laboratories formed the core CSIR chain established largely through Bhatnagar's efforts in his lifetime?
options: About a dozen national laboratories and institutes
None; they were all built after his death
questions.explanation: Bhatnagar spearheaded the creation of around a dozen national laboratories and institutes that formed the backbone of the CSIR network.
